signal.summary: With only 10 minutes remaining until expiry, the live spot price of 63,616.7 has slipped below the strike price of 63,622. Recent 15-minute momentum is negative at -0.16% and the RSI of 37.77 indicates short-term weakness, favoring a finish below the strike. While high realized volatility of 55.50% means a quick reversal is possible, the immediate trend and current spot deficit point downward.

With the live spot price at 63,616.7, BTC is currently trading just below the strike price of 63,622 with only 10 minutes remaining to expiry. Recent 15-minute momentum is negative at -0.16% and the RSI indicates bearishness at 37.77, suggesting continued short-term downward pressure. Although the distance to the strike is very small, the current spot deficit and negative momentum favor a 'down' settlement.

The current spot price of 63,616.7 has fallen below the strike of 63,622, and the negative 15-minute return of -0.16% indicates short-term downward momentum. With only 10 minutes remaining to expiry, the market-implied P(up) of 51.46% overprices the upward outcome relative to the spot deficit. Taking a down position offers a clear statistical edge given the overpriced premium and bearish immediate trend.

BTC spot has slipped to 63,616.7, placing it below the strike of 63,622 with only 10 minutes remaining until expiry. Recent 15-minute momentum is negative (-0.16%) and broader sentiment remains in Extreme Fear (23), indicating continued short-term downward pressure. While the gap is small relative to 10-minute volatility, the current deficit and negative momentum favor a down finish.

debate.bull: Although BTC is currently trading slightly below the strike at 63,616.7, the deficit is a minuscule $5.30 (less than 0.01%), which can be easily overcome in seconds given the high realized volatility of 55.50%. Furthermore, the short-term 15-minute pullback has pushed the RSI down to 37.77, signaling that the asset is approaching oversold territory and is ripe for a quick technical rebound. Additionally, the positive 1-hour return of 0.17% demonstrates that the broader hourly uptrend remains intact, providing the necessary supportive drift. With the risk-neutral baseline probability of finishing up already at 50.01%, even a minor upward fluctuation before the 10-minute expiry will comfortably push the price above the strike.
debate.bear: With only 10 minutes remaining until expiry, BTC is already trading below the strike price at 63,616.7, requiring an immediate reversal against prevailing short-term downward momentum. Recent 15-minute returns of -0.16% and a weak RSI of 37.77 underscore immediate bearish pressure, further compounded by broader market sentiment sitting in "Extreme Fear" (23). Furthermore, the market-implied P(up) of 51.46% significantly overprices the probability of an upward finish given the spot deficit, creating a highly favorable statistical edge for a down settlement.

keyDrivers: Spot deficit of $5.30 relative to strike with only 10 minutes to expiry
Negative short-term momentum (15m return of -0.16% and RSI at 37.77)
Overpriced market-implied probability of 51.46% compared to a driftless baseline of ~48.6%
proposal.reasoning: With only 10 minutes remaining, BTC trades at $63,616.7, just below the strike of $63,622. Although the deficit is small ($5.30), the negative 15-minute momentum and bearish analyst consensus favor a down finish. Given a standard deviation of ~$154 over this period, the probability of crossing the strike is around 48.6% under a random walk, making the market-implied P(up) of 51.46% overpriced and presenting a clear edge for the down outcome.

plan.thesis: With only 10 minutes remaining, BTC trades at $63,616.7, just below the strike of $63,622. Although the deficit is small ($5.30), the negative 15-minute momentum and bearish analyst consensus favor a down finish. Given a standard deviation of ~$154 over this period, the probability of crossing the strike is around 48.6% under a random walk, making the market-implied P(up) of 51.46% overpriced and presenting a clear edge for the down outcome. — DOWN edge 5.5% (subjective 54.0% vs market 48.5%), stake 2.7% of bankroll

verdict.reasoning: All circuit breakers passed; plan approved as sized.
